items.title: Toward a science of prospective learning
items.author: Konrad P. Kording, Joshua T. Vogelstein, Pratik Chaudhari, Timothy Verstynen
items.content: <article>In a constantly changing world, effective intelligence means anticipating future changes. Kording et al. argue that organisms adapt prospectively, modeling how environments and capabilities of the organism evolve, thus optimizing decisions for tomorrow’s unpredictable world.</article>

items.title: PsychENCODE at 10: From genomic maps to mechanistic insights in mental illness
items.author: Panos Roussos, Flora M. Vaccarino, Zhiping Weng, Nenad Sestan, Mark Gerstein, Daniel H. Geschwind
items.content: <article>Over the past decade, the PsychENCODE Consortium has transformed psychiatric genomics—from static maps of genetic risk to dynamic, cell-resolved models of the human brain—linking DNA sequence to neural circuitry and behavior and laying the foundation for precision approaches to mental illness.</article>

items.title: Cortical state dynamics as a target for stroke motor rehabilitation
items.author: Aamir Abbasi, Tanuj Gulati
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Choi et al. demonstrate that stroke disrupts cortical state transitions underlying reach-to-grasp control. Recovery depends on restoring separability between beta bursts and execution-related co-firing, a process enhanced by low-frequency stimulation that promotes motor recovery.</article>

items.title: What is a ripple: Toward a grammar of memory replay
items.author: Liset M. de la Prida
items.content: <article>Ripples support memory consolidation via neuronal replay. Castelli et al. identify two ripple types with distinct inputs and dynamics. This Preview discusses their findings in the context of current views on ripple diversity and hypothesizes that such diversity reflects a replay grammar.</article>

items.title: PV interneurons gate sex-specific winner effects
items.author: Xinli Song, Yu Chen, Kun Li
items.content: <article>Winner effects, where past victories enhance success, shape competition. In this issue of Neuron, Zheng et al. show that female mice form hierarchies with weaker winner effects because prefrontal interneurons limit thalamic-prefrontal synaptic strengthening, revealing a framework for sex differences in social dominance.</article>

items.title: Astrocyte in stroke: Location matters
items.author: Fu-Dong Shi
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Gleichman et al. reveal that astrocytes exhibit region-specific features after stroke. Cortical astrocytes, but not white matter astrocytes, leverage Lamc1 to promote vascular remodeling, highlighting the complexity of harnessing astrocytes for neurorepair.</article>

items.title: Semaphorin–Neuropilin Interactions Underlying Sympathetic Axon Responses to Class III Semaphorins
items.author: Hang Chen, Zhigang He, Anil Bagri, Marc Tessier-Lavigne
